The quality or state of being negligent; lack of due diligence or care.
The estimated reparation in money for detriment or injury sustained.
That which corrects or counteracts an evil of any kind.

The transfer of a cause from a court of inferior to one of superior jurisdiction.
A writ having for its object to bring a party before a court or judge.
Law in its regular course of administration through courts of justice.
The right of the nation or state to take private property for public use.
The supreme power in a state.
German: compulsory share of inheritance. Children receive 50% of intestate share.
French: hereditary reserve. 50-75% of estate reserved for children.
Dutch: legitimate portion. Children's right to 50% of intestate share.
Swedish: legal share. Children's right to 50% of intestate share.
Japanese: retained portion. Children's compulsory share, 50% of intestate.
Korean: retained portion. Children's compulsory share.
Islamic: obligatory inheritance shares prescribed by Quran (4:11-12).
German: care-right. Joint parental care is the default.
Swedish: shared care. Joint custody is the default.
French: parental authority. Joint exercise is the principle.
Islamic: physical custody of child. Maternal preference for young children.
Islamic: legal guardianship/authority over child. Paternal and permanent.
Swedish: contact sabotage. Statutory offense of undermining child's relationship.
German: remaining-debt-liberation. Discharge after 3-year good conduct.
Roman: loss of liberty, citizenship, and family rights. Total legal death.
Roman: loss of citizenship and family rights, liberty retained.
Roman: loss of family rights only.
Spanish/Italian: legitimate share for forced heirs.
Arabic: one who is unable to pay. Entitled to respite rather than punishment.
